question.content: For the beam decomposed into p_0, R_D, and M_D subproblems, solve the right-end vertical reaction R_D using end displacement and rotation compatibility. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression for the target quantity. Use the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use direct integration of the Euler-Bernoulli equation for one beam with unknown R_D and M_D.
step_1.key_point: Solve a single field model instead of three superposed load cases.

step_3.content: Write E*I*v"=M(x) on each x-region, integrate to slope and deflection, and keep integration constants.
step_3.key_point: Continuity at the half-span links constants between the two regions.

step_5.content: Apply fixed-end constraints v(0)=0, theta(0)=0, v(L)=0, theta(L)=0 to solve constants together with R_D and M_D.
step_5.key_point: Boundary conditions directly determine the reaction unknowns.

step_7.content: Extract R_D from the solved system and reduce it to the target expression.
step_7.key_point: Differential-equation integration gives the final reaction in one route.
